steps.instruction: To compare, first count each group. Group A: 🐱🐱🐱🐱🐱 Group B: 🐱🐱🐱
steps.work: Group A: 5 cats. Group B: 3 cats.
steps.explanation: We need to know how many are in each group before we can compare.
When we count, 5 comes after 3, so 5 is the bigger number.

steps.instruction: The group with the bigger number has more.
steps.work: Group A has more because 5 is more than 3.
steps.explanation: 5 > 3, so Group A (5 cats) has more than Group B (3 cats).

title: Matching to See If Groups Are Equal

steps.instruction: Place objects from each group side by side. Group A: 🔵🔵🔵🔵 Group B: 🔴🔴🔴🔴
steps.explanation: Matching objects one-to-one helps us see if the groups are the same size.

steps.instruction: Are there any objects without a match?
steps.work: No leftovers! Every blue has a red match.
steps.explanation: If nothing is left over, the groups have the same number.

steps.instruction: Since every object has a match, the groups are equal.
steps.explanation: Both groups have 4 objects. When two groups have the same count, we say they are equal.
